text: Equity Plan Management
Domain Knowledge & Terminology Guide
Introduction

Equity plan management encompasses the administration, tracking, and compliance activities related to employee equity compensation programs. These programs include stock options, restricted stock units, performance shares, and other equity-based awards that companies use to attract, retain, and incentivize employees.
This document provides comprehensive terminology and domain knowledge essential for understanding equity plan management systems and applications.
Core Equity Instruments
Stock Options
Stock options give employees the right to purchase company shares at a predetermined price within a specific timeframe.
Incentive Stock Options (ISO)
Tax-qualified stock options available only to employees
Potential for favorable capital gains tax treatment if holding period requirements are met
Subject to $100,000 annual exercisability limit based on grant date fair market value
May trigger Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) upon exercise
Must be granted at or above fair market value on grant date
Non-Qualified Stock Options (NSO/NQSO)
Can be granted to employees, directors, contractors, and consultants
Spread between exercise price and fair market value taxed as ordinary income upon exercise
No $100,000 limit or AMT concerns
Can be granted at any strike price (though typically at fair market value)
Subject to income tax withholding requirements at exercise
Restricted Stock Units (RSUs)
A promise to deliver shares in the future upon satisfaction of vesting conditions. No purchase required by the employee.
Taxed as ordinary income when shares are delivered upon vesting
Common settlement methods: net settlement, sell-to-cover, or same-day sale
No exercise price or decision required from participant
May include dividend equivalents that accrue during vesting period
Restricted Stock Awards (RSAs)
Actual shares granted upfront subject to vesting conditions and potential forfeiture.
Shareholder rights (voting, dividends) typically granted immediately
Taxed at grant or vesting depending on IRC Section 83(b) election
Section 83(b) election allows taxation at grant instead of vesting, potentially at lower value
Unvested shares subject to forfeiture if service terminates
Performance Shares/Units (PSUs)
Equity awards that vest based on achievement of pre-established performance goals over a specified period.
Performance metrics may include revenue, earnings, total shareholder return, or other KPIs
Payout can range from 0% to 200% (or more) of target based on performance
Often includes threshold, target, and maximum performance levels
Performance period typically 1-3 years
Stock Appreciation Rights (SARs)
The right to receive the appreciation in stock value between grant and exercise, settled in cash or stock.
Similar economics to stock options without requiring purchase
Can be settled in cash, shares, or combination
Tandem SARs: granted alongside stock options, exercisable alternatively
Standalone SARs: independent instruments not linked to options
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
A program allowing employees to purchase company stock through payroll deductions, often at a discount.
Qualified ESPPs under IRC Section 423 receive favorable tax treatment
Offering periods typically 6 months to 2 years
Purchase price discount up to 15% allowed
Lookback provision: discount applied to lower of price at offering start or purchase date
Annual contribution limit of $25,000 (based on fair market value)
Key Terminology
Grant Terms
Grant Date
The date on which the equity award is formally approved and awarded to the participant. This date determines the fair market value for accounting and tax purposes and starts vesting schedules.
Fair Market Value (FMV)

The price of the company's stock as determined by the plan administrator, typically using the closing price on the grant date for public companies or an independent valuation (409A) for private companies.
Strike Price / Exercise Price
The predetermined price at which an option holder can purchase shares when exercising their stock options. For ISOs, must be at least equal to FMV at grant date.
Vesting Schedule
The timeline over which equity awards become exercisable or non-forfeitable. Common schedules include:
Cliff vesting: 100% vests at a single point (e.g., 1-year cliff)
Graded/ratable vesting: portions vest periodically (e.g., 25% per year over 4 years)
Monthly vesting: continuous vesting each month
Performance-based: vests upon achieving specific milestones
Vesting Commencement Date
The date from which vesting begins to accrue, may differ from grant date. Often tied to hire date or service start date.
Expiration / Termination Date
The final date by which options must be exercised or they will be forfeited. Typically 10 years from grant date for ISOs and NSOs, but may be shortened upon termination of service.
Transaction Terms
Exercise
The act of purchasing shares by paying the exercise price for vested stock options. Exercise methods include:
Cash exercise: paying exercise price in cash
Cashless/same-day sale: exercising and immediately selling shares to cover costs
Stock swap: using already-owned shares to pay exercise price
Net exercise: company withholds shares to cover exercise price
Settlement
The delivery of shares following vesting of RSUs or exercise of options. Settlement timing may be immediate, delayed, or at participant election.
Release
The process of removing restrictions on shares, allowing them to be freely traded. For restricted stock, this occurs when vesting conditions are satisfied.
Forfeiture
The loss of unvested equity awards, typically occurring when employment terminates before vesting is complete or when performance conditions are not met.
Cancellation
The termination of equity awards by the company or participant, which may be voluntary or involuntary. Vested options not exercised by expiration date are cancelled.
Tax and Compliance Terminology
Tax Events and Treatment
Bargain Element / Spread
The difference between the fair market value of stock and the exercise price paid. This amount represents taxable income for NSOs at exercise.
Disqualifying Disposition
Sale of ISO shares that does not meet the required holding periods (1 year from exercise and 2 years from grant), causing the transaction to be taxed as ordinary income rather than capital gains.
Qualifying Disposition
Sale of ISO shares after meeting both holding period requirements, allowing favorable long-term capital gains tax treatment on the entire gain.
Withholding
Tax amounts deducted from equity compensation to satisfy federal, state, and local income tax obligations. Methods include:
Share withholding: company retains shares to cover tax liability
Cash payment: participant pays taxes in cash
Sell-to-cover: broker sells sufficient shares to cover taxes
IRC Section 409A

IRS regulations governing deferred compensation, including equity awards. Requires that options be granted at fair market value and that deferred compensation comply with strict distribution rules. Violations result in immediate taxation and 20% penalty.
409A Valuation
Independent appraisal of a private company's common stock value, required for determining FMV for option grants and ensuring 409A compliance. Typically valid for 12 months or until a material event.
IRC Section 162(m)
Tax code provision limiting corporate tax deductions for compensation over $1 million paid to certain executives, affecting how public companies structure equity compensation.
IRC Section 280G
Governs golden parachute payments (excess payments in connection with change in control). Equity awards may be subject to excise taxes if they constitute excess parachute payments.
Regulatory Compliance
ASC 718 / FAS 123R
Accounting standard requiring companies to measure and recognize compensation expense for equity awards based on grant date fair value, typically expensed over the vesting period.
Grant Date Fair Value
The value assigned to an equity award for accounting purposes, calculated using valuation models such as Black-Scholes for options or market price for full-value awards.
Section 16
Securities Exchange Act provisions requiring corporate insiders (officers, directors, 10% shareholders) to report transactions and potentially disgorge short-swing profits.
Rule 10b5-1 Plan
Pre-established trading plan allowing insiders to sell stock at predetermined times or prices, providing an affirmative defense against insider trading allegations.
Blackout Period
Time period during which certain individuals (typically insiders) are prohibited from trading company stock, often surrounding earnings announcements or other material events.
Plan Administration
Plan Structure
Share Reserve / Pool
The total number of shares authorized for issuance under an equity plan. Plans typically have fixed pools that may be replenished through share recycling or shareholder approval.
Evergreen Provision
An automatic annual increase to the share pool, typically calculated as a percentage of outstanding shares. Common in private company plans.
Share Recycling
The return of shares to the plan's available pool under certain circumstances, such as:
Forfeiture of unvested awards
Expiration of unexercised options
Cancellation or cash settlement of awards
Note: Shares used for tax withholding or option exercise typically do NOT return to pool
Dilution
The reduction in existing shareholders' ownership percentage resulting from issuance of new equity awards. Measured by overhang and annual burn rate metrics.
Overhang

Total potential dilution from equity compensation, calculated as (outstanding equity awards + shares available for grant) divided by (fully diluted shares outstanding). Institutional investors monitor this metric closely.
Burn Rate
The annual rate at which a company grants equity awards, typically expressed as a percentage of shares outstanding. Calculated as (shares granted in year) / (weighted average shares outstanding).
Plan Provisions
Change in Control (CIC)
Event triggering special provisions in equity plans, typically including merger, acquisition, or change in board majority. May result in:
Accelerated vesting of equity awards
Cash-out of awards
Assumption or substitution of awards by acquirer
Single-Trigger Acceleration
Vesting acceleration that occurs upon a change in control event alone, without requiring termination of employment.
Double-Trigger Acceleration

Vesting acceleration requiring both a change in control AND termination without cause or for good reason within a specified period (typically 12-18 months). More common and shareholder-friendly than single-trigger.
Clawback / Recoupment

Company's right to recover equity compensation under certain circumstances, such as financial restatement, violation of restrictive covenants, or misconduct. Required by SEC rules for public companies.
Post-Termination Exercise Period (PTEP)
The time period after termination during which vested options remain exercisable. Typical periods:
90 days for voluntary resignation or termination for cause
12 months for termination due to death or disability
Extended periods may be offered for retirement-eligible employees
Corporate Actions and Events
Equity Adjustments
Stock Split
Division of existing shares into multiple shares. For example, a 2-for-1 split doubles share count and halves price. Equity awards are proportionally adjusted to maintain economic value.
Reverse Stock Split
Consolidation of shares at a specified ratio (e.g., 1-for-5), reducing share count and increasing per-share price. Awards adjusted proportionally.
Dividend
Distribution of earnings to shareholders. Cash dividends generally do not affect equity awards, but plans may provide dividend equivalents that track dividends paid on underlying shares.
Stock Dividend / Stock Distribution
Payment of dividend in shares rather than cash. Awards are adjusted to reflect the additional shares received.
Spin-off
Creation of independent company through distribution of subsidiary shares to existing shareholders. Parent company awards may be adjusted or supplemented with subsidiary awards to preserve value.
Recapitalization / Reorganization
Restructuring of company's capital structure, potentially involving exchange of securities. Equity awards adjusted to reflect new capital structure while maintaining intrinsic value.
System and Operational Terminology
Data Management
Participant
Individual who has been granted equity awards. Also referred to as optionee, grantee, or award holder.
Grant / Award ID
Unique identifier assigned to each equity grant for tracking and administrative purposes.
Lot
A distinct group of shares from a specific grant with unique tax characteristics. Important for tax lot accounting and determining cost basis for sales.
Position
Current status of a participant's equity holdings, including grants, vested shares, exercisable options, and shares owned.
Transaction History
Complete record of all events affecting equity awards, including grants, vesting events, exercises, releases, forfeitures, and sales. Essential audit trail for compliance and reporting.
Reporting and Analytics
Gain Report
Analysis showing unrealized or realized gains on equity awards, calculated as difference between current/sale price and exercise/grant price.
Equity Compensation Expense
Accounting charge recognized over vesting period based on grant date fair value of equity awards, required under ASC 718.
Underwater Options
Stock options with exercise price higher than current fair market value, making them economically worthless until stock price increases. Also called 'out-of-the-money' options.
In-the-Money Options
Options with exercise price below current fair market value, having intrinsic value equal to the spread.
Equity Award Summary
Participant-facing statement showing all equity holdings, including grant details, vesting schedules, exercisable quantities, and current values.
System Features
Participant Portal / Self-Service
Online platform where participants can view equity holdings, execute transactions, update personal information, and access tax documents.
Automated Workflows
System-driven processes for routine tasks such as vesting calculations, exercise processing, tax withholding, and regulatory filings.
Integration
Connection between equity management system and other enterprise systems:
HRIS (employee data, terminations, job changes)
Payroll (tax withholding, W-2 reporting)
General ledger (expense recognition, journal entries)
Transfer agent (share issuance, certificates)
Brokerage (trading, settlement)
Broker-Assisted Transactions
Exercise and sale transactions facilitated through integrated brokerage platform, enabling cashless exercises and same-day sales.
Document Generation
Automated creation of grant agreements, exercise confirmations, tax forms, and participant communications based on system data.
Modeling and Forecasting
Tools for projecting future dilution, expense, cash flow, and share usage under various grant scenarios and assumptions.
Best Practices and Considerations
Data Quality and Governance
Maintain single source of truth for all equity data
Implement robust reconciliation processes between equity system and general ledger
Document all plan interpretations and administrative decisions
Establish clear approval hierarchies and segregation of duties
Regular audit trails and exception reporting
Compliance Management
Stay current with evolving tax, accounting, and securities regulations
Coordinate with legal, tax, and finance teams on plan design changes
Ensure timely and accurate regulatory filings (Form 4, Form 5, proxy disclosure)
Monitor insider trading compliance and blackout period adherence
Validate 409A valuations and compliance for private companies
Participant Experience
Provide clear, accessible education on equity compensation
Ensure participant portal is intuitive and mobile-friendly
Deliver timely notifications for vesting events and expiration warnings
Offer decision-support tools for exercise and tax planning
Provide responsive customer support for transaction questions
System Selection and Implementation
Evaluate scalability to support company growth and complexity
Assess integration capabilities with existing enterprise systems
Consider global capabilities for international equity programs
Validate reporting and analytics functionality
Ensure vendor provides regular regulatory updates and system enhancements
Plan for thorough data migration and validation during implementation
Company Profile: Vijaya_equity

Vijaya_equity is a leading equity plan management firm specializing in end-to-end administration of employee equity compensation programs for both private and public companies. Founded with a mission to simplify complex equity processes, Vijaya_equity combines deep domain expertise with modern technology to deliver accurate, compliant, and participant-friendly equity management solutions.
How Vijaya_equity Executes Equity Plan Management
Grant Administration

Vijaya_equity manages the full lifecycle of equity grants, including stock options (ISO and NSO), RSUs, RSAs, PSUs, SARs, and ESPPs. Upon receiving board-approved grant data, the team validates grant details against plan documents, confirms fair market value using either exchange closing prices or current 409A valuations for private companies, and uploads all grant records into the equity management platform. Grant agreements are generated and distributed electronically, with participant acknowledgment tracked within the system.
Vesting and Compliance Tracking

Vijaya_equity configures and monitors vesting schedules for all award types, including cliff, graded, monthly, and performance-based vesting. The team coordinates with the client’s HRIS to capture employment events such as terminations, leaves of absence, and role changes, and adjusts vesting accordingly. Automated alerts notify administrators and participants of upcoming vesting milestones and expiration dates. For PSU awards, Vijaya_equity works with the compensation committee to determine performance outcomes at the end of each performance period and processes payouts based on certified results.
Transaction Processing

Vijaya_equity processes all equity transactions including option exercises (cash, cashless, net exercise, and stock swap), RSU releases, RSA lapse of restrictions, and ESPP purchases. Each transaction is reviewed for compliance with plan terms and insider trading policies, including blackout period checks and Section 16 reporting requirements. Tax withholding calculations are performed for each event using current supplemental tax rates, and tax proceeds are remitted to payroll in coordination with the client’s payroll provider.
Tax and Regulatory Compliance

Vijaya_equity maintains strict compliance with applicable tax codes and securities regulations. The team prepares year-end tax reporting including Form W-2 supplemental income reconciliation, Form 1099-B for option exercises and RSU sales, and ISO qualifying and disqualifying disposition tracking. For public company clients, Vijaya_equity supports Section 16 reporting by preparing Form 4 filings and maintaining Rule 10b5-1 plan records. The firm also ensures ASC 718 expense recognition data is delivered to finance teams on a monthly basis, aligned with grant date fair values and vesting amortization schedules.
Participant Experience and Support

Vijaya_equity operates a secure participant portal where employees can view their equity holdings, initiate transactions, access vesting schedules, and download tax documents. The portal features real-time stock price data, gain modeling tools, and mobile-friendly design to ensure accessibility across devices. A dedicated support team handles participant inquiries regarding grant details, exercise mechanics, and tax implications, providing timely and accurate responses with escalation paths to senior administrators for complex issues.
Corporate Actions and Plan Governance

Vijaya_equity manages equity award adjustments resulting from corporate actions such as stock splits, reverse splits, spin-offs, and mergers or acquisitions. In change-in-control scenarios, the team evaluates plan provisions to determine whether single-trigger or double-trigger acceleration applies, coordinates cash-out or assumption of awards, and communicates clearly with participants throughout the transition. Share pool reconciliations, burn rate analysis, and overhang reporting are delivered quarterly to the board and compensation committee to support plan governance decisions.
Technology and Integration

Vijaya_equity’s platform integrates with leading HRIS, payroll, general ledger, transfer agent, and brokerage systems, enabling seamless data flow across the enterprise. Automated workflows reduce manual intervention and associated errors, while comprehensive audit trails and exception reports support internal controls and external audits. The firm continuously invests in platform enhancements to reflect regulatory changes, ensuring clients remain compliant as rules evolve.
Conclusion

Effective equity plan management requires deep understanding of complex instruments, regulations, tax implications, and administrative processes. Modern equity management systems must balance sophisticated functionality with ease of use, ensuring compliance while delivering value to both companies and participants.

This terminology guide serves as a foundation for understanding the equity compensation domain. Practitioners should stay informed of regulatory changes, emerging best practices, and evolving technology capabilities to effectively manage equity programs in an increasingly complex environment.
